bounded_contexts.name: Payment Processing
bounded_contexts.description: Core domain: processes Pix, Card, and Boleto charges through the provider abstraction layer

bounded_contexts.name: Provider Abstraction
bounded_contexts.description: Provider Pattern — abstracts internal simulation vs external gateway (Asaas). Feature-flag switchable at runtime.

bounded_contexts.name: Card Vault
bounded_contexts.description: Tokenized card storage. Never persists full card numbers. Cross-app reusable tokens.

bounded_contexts.name: Split Engine
bounded_contexts.description: Payment split logic for multi-party transactions (platform + vendor + delivery)

bounded_contexts.name: Webhook & Callback
bounded_contexts.description: Inbound webhook processing (from Asaas) and outbound callback delivery (to ecosystem apps) with retry logic

bounded_contexts.name: Admin Panel
bounded_contexts.description: Supporting subdomain: admin dashboard, transaction management, provider config, feature flags, audit logs

deliverables.project_structure: see ecp-pay/ folder — 50+ files created following tech_spec.md Section 6 exactly

endpoints.description: Issue boleto with barcode + Pix QR

adrs.title: Provider Pattern for payment gateway abstraction
adrs.context: ECP ecosystem needs to work offline (dev/staging) and with real gateways (production). Apps should not know which gateway is active.
adrs.decision: Implement PaymentProvider interface with two adapters (InternalAdapter, AsaasAdapter). Factory selects adapter based on feature flag PAYMENT_PROVIDER. Flag switchable at runtime via admin panel.
adrs.consequences: All payment operations go through a single interface. Adding a new gateway (e.g., Stripe) means implementing one adapter class. No changes to consuming apps.

adrs.title: SQLite as single-file embedded database
adrs.context: ECP Pay is a monorepo service used in local dev, staging, and small-scale production. Need zero-config database.
adrs.decision: Use better-sqlite3 with WAL mode. Single file database-pay.sqlite. Migrations run on startup.
adrs.consequences: No external DB server needed. Single npm install and the service runs. WAL mode allows concurrent reads. Trade-off: not suitable for high-scale horizontal deployment.

adrs.title: Dual authentication: API Key for apps, JWT for admin
adrs.context: Two distinct user groups: ecosystem apps (machine-to-machine) and admin humans (web panel).
adrs.decision: Apps authenticate via X-API-Key header checked against app_registrations table. Admin users authenticate via JWT issued at login. Separate middleware for each.
adrs.consequences: Clean separation of concerns. Apps never need user credentials. Admin panel has proper session management with role-based access control.

adrs.title: Idempotency via unique key per transaction
adrs.context: Network failures can cause duplicate payment requests. Must ensure a payment is created exactly once.
adrs.decision: Require X-Idempotency-Key header on all mutating payment endpoints. Store as UNIQUE column in transactions table. On duplicate key, return existing result.
adrs.consequences: Safe retries for all apps. UNIQUE constraint prevents double-charging at the database level.

adrs.title: Card data never persisted — token-only vault
adrs.context: PCI compliance and security: raw card numbers and CVVs must not be stored anywhere.
adrs.decision: Only store token + last4 + brand + holder_name in card_tokens table. Raw card data is passed to the provider and immediately discarded.
adrs.consequences: Secure by design. Token is reusable across all ecosystem apps for the same customer. Trade-off: cannot re-display full card number.

adrs.title: Webhook deduplication via event_id
adrs.context: External providers (Asaas) may retry webhook delivery, causing duplicate processing.
adrs.decision: Store event_id as UNIQUE in webhook_events table. Check before processing. If duplicate, return 200 without re-processing.
adrs.consequences: Idempotent webhook handling. No duplicate status changes or callbacks.

technical_constraints: TypeScript strict mode — no 'any', no implicit nulls
All monetary values in centavos (integer) — NEVER float
UUID v4 for all primary keys
AppError class for all known errors — structured JSON response
Soft delete only — no physical deletion of records
Every transaction records source_app (which ecosystem app originated it)
Provider interface is the ONLY way to execute payment operations
Feature flag PAYMENT_PROVIDER switchable at runtime without restart
Internal and external modes return identical response payloads
Audit log for all administrative actions
